module.title: SAP TM Freight Settlement & Charge Management

learningObjectives.title: Understand cost distribution
learningObjectives.description: Explain how total freight cost is allocated across shipments or business objects.

learningObjectives.title: Understand allocation methods
learningObjectives.description: Describe weight, volume, quantity, value, distance, and equal split rules.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate from charge calculation
learningObjectives.description: Clarify total cost calculation versus post-calculation allocation outcomes.

sections.content: Cost Distribution and Cost Allocation determine how transportation costs are divided among Freight Units, deliveries, sales orders or business organizations. Instead of assigning the entire transportation cost to a single shipment, SAP TM distributes costs according to predefined business rules such as weight, volume, quantity, distance or value.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Cost Distribution answers the question: 'How should the total transportation cost be shared among multiple shipments or business objects?'

Three Freight Units share one truck from Pune to Delhi. The total transportation cost is ₹60,000. SAP TM distributes the cost based on shipment weight: the heaviest shipment receives ₹30,000, the medium shipment ₹20,000 and the lightest shipment ₹10,000.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Select allocation rules that reflect actual business operations. Weight-based allocation is common for manufacturing, while value-based allocation is often used for high-value products.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: An FMCG company distributes transportation costs across more than 8,000 retail deliveries every day based on shipment weight. This enables accurate profitability reporting for each customer and product category.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Certification questions often ask the difference between Charge Calculation and Cost Distribution. First SAP TM calculates the total freight charge, then it distributes that cost to the relevant business objects.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Cost Distribution does not change the total freight charge. It only allocates the already calculated transportation cost among different business objects.

items: Allocation can be based on weight, volume, value, quantity or distance.
Charge Calculation occurs before Cost Distribution.
Cost Allocation supports profitability and financial reporting.
The total transportation cost remains unchanged after distribution.
